note: Authoritative local data from L1 chains.db (DeFiLlama + CoinGecko + growthepie/Dune). Raw signals, no scores. null = NO DATA (do not guess). FRESHNESS: price & market caps are live (today); TVL/stablecoin LEVELS are the latest available (carried forward if a chain's today-point isn't in yet); daily-FLOW metrics (DEX volume, fees, active_addresses, tx_count) are the LAST COMPLETE UTC DAY (today is partial and excluded) — full-day figures, use them as-is. SHARES are each chain's % of the WHOLE DeFi market (all ~500 chains; denominators in market.total_*), so tracked chains sum to <100% and deltas are immune to which chains we track. TVL/volume are USD-denominated, so share moves in a big market swing partly reflect token price, not pure flows. Each chain ALSO carries its OWN raw TVL growth — tvl_change_1d/7d/30d_pct (chain's level vs itself N days ago); use THESE for 'is this chain's TVL growing', NOT the share deltas (share = slice of market, a different question). If tvl_suspect_1d is true the 1d move is a likely upstream data glitch (a >25% one-day swing) — discount the 1d figure and lean on the 7d/30d trend instead.

emerging_candidates.method: longshot-style L1/L2 momentum + DefiLlama TVL growth, radar excluded
emerging_candidates.how_to_use: RAW watchlist of non-radar chains rising on the signals below — NOT buy calls. A candidate becomes a LEAD only on confluence (also trending / in a heating narrative / has a catalyst / fresh dev-gov) or a confirmed on-chain TVL surge.

signal_glossary.volume_heat: Volume is large vs market cap (turnover ≥15%) — unusual interest building.
signal_glossary.vol_leads_price: Volume is hot but 7d price hasn't run yet — interest building BEFORE the move (the early sweet spot).
signal_glossary.momentum: 7d gain keeping pace with / exceeding 30d — the move is accelerating, not fading.
signal_glossary.low_float: Only a small share of supply circulates — CAUTION: unlock overhang can cap upside.
signal_glossary.tvl_surge: On-chain TVL jumped ≥25% in 7d from a small base — real capital arriving, not just token price.

narrative.method: CoinGecko category momentum (24h mcap change + constituent 7d breadth/median); thematic narratives only (chain-ecosystem/stablecoin categories excluded)

events.subject: FTSE Russell 1000 reconstitution (Bitmine/ETH)
events.note: ~$2.15B passive ETH buying; effective date. Watch ETH 5-7d before.

events.subject: Hyperliquid HYPE monthly unlock
events.note: ~2.5% of supply, monthly cadence on the 6th through 2027

events.subject: Plasma (XPL) US-purchaser unlock cliff
events.note: 2.5B XPL = 25% of total supply; zero-fee chain has no offsetting capture

catalysts.note: Curated dated catalysts (edit catalysts.json). DeFiLlama unlocks API is Pro-gated; the routine news-scan supplements. Fold these into the Open Items ledger.

attention.source: CoinGecko /search/trending (search/view velocity = retail attention proxy; LunarCrush free tier is locked/402)

pre_winner_lookalikes.method: validated winner-DNA coil screen (deep-below-high + oversold + at-30d-low [all validated, hold OOS] + below-ATH/high-vol/mid-float/small-cap); primed-but-not-yet-moved. NECESSARY-not-sufficient setup — needs an event trigger.
pre_winner_lookalikes.how_to_use: Beaten-down coils matching the data-validated winner DNA. SETUPS, not buys: most never move. Edge comes ONLY from confluence — a lookalike that is also trending / in a heating narrative / has a near catalyst / has fresh dev-gov.

signal_glossary.deep_below_60d_high: Trading <50% of its 60-day high — deeply beaten down. VALIDATED (held out-of-sample).
signal_glossary.oversold_30d: Down >25% over 30 days — oversold. VALIDATED (held out-of-sample).
signal_glossary.at_30d_low: Trading within ~3% of its 30-day low RIGHT NOW — the capitulation launch zone. VALIDATED (held out-of-sample); a bounce off the low actually LOWERS winner odds, so this is a timing edge.
signal_glossary.oversold_90d: Down >40% over 90 days — sustained downtrend (supporting context).
signal_glossary.deep_below_ath: Trading <15% of all-time high — far from prior peak, room to recover.
signal_glossary.high_vol: Elevated 30d volatility — capable of large moves.
signal_glossary.mid_float: Meaningful but not-fully-diluted float — the winner sweet spot.
signal_glossary.small_cap: Sub-$100M market cap — small enough to multiply.

dev_governance.note: Pre-announcement layer (early, low-crowd): GitHub releases/commit-activity + Snapshot governance for tracked protocols. A fresh_release or a pending 'launch/fund X' proposal precedes the marketing post. Each item carries release_notes / body (truncated) + a keyword dimension_hint (raw, NOT a verdict) — the FOLLOW-UP is to read these and judge what the change actually does and whether it improves the protocol at a fundamental level (consensus/scaling/security/economics/new capability) vs routine housekeeping. No composite score.

dev.release_notes:
## Protocol
#### Sui Protocol Version in this release: `128`

https://github.com/MystenLabs/sui/pull/27097: Introduces a new protocol version that adds explicit bounds for binary pools that were otherwise implicitly constrained before. No user-visible change.

https://github.com/MystenLabs/sui/pull/26896: added protocol version 127 enabling `always_advance_dkg_to_resolution`. Corrects version 126 which was incorrectly modified after being released to testnet.

https://github.com/MystenLabs/sui/pull/26901: Updated gas prices for range proof verification and Ristretto group operations in protocol version 127.

https://github.com/MystenLabs/sui/pull/26998: Enable Ristretto255 group operations and bulletproofs range proof verification on testnet.

https://github.com/MystenLabs/sui/pull/26996: Mainnet switches to timestamp-based epoch close at protocol version 127. No operator action required.

## Nodes (Validators and Full nodes)

https://github.com/MystenLabs/sui/pull/26973: A node now reports and disconnects state-sync peers that are consistently unreachable even when it is not actively syncing from them; previously this could fail to happen. No operator action required.

https://github.com/MystenLabs/sui/pull/26989: Fixes a spurious panic that could occur during a normal node shutdown/restart. No action required.

## gRPC

https://github.com/MystenLabs/sui/pull/26763: \`SimulateTransaction\` now accepts \`gas_price=0\` for gasless-tier-eligible transactions (both via the …[truncated]

This release is available as a Docker Image on Docker Hub at `offchainlabs/nitro-node:v3.11.1-8512b8c`
This Docker image specifies default flags in its entrypoint which should be replicated if you're overriding the entrypoint: `/usr/local/bin/nitro --validation.wasm.allowed-wasm-module-roots /home/user/nitro-legacy/machines,/home/user/target/machines\`

If you're running a validator without a split validation server (this will be true of most validators), you should instead use the image offchainlabs/nitro-node:v3.11.1-8512b8c-validator which has the extra script `/usr/local/bin/split-val-entry.sh` as the entrypoint.

## [v3.11.1](https://github.com/OffchainLabs/nitro/compare/v3.11.0...v3.11.1) - 2026-06-29

### Fixed

- The Stylus warm-start cache is now rolled back when a transaction is dropped after executing. [[commit]](https://github.com/OffchainLabs/nitro/commit/860a15a8b83e0873732c67abd1593653500fb7e9)
- Use mutex for history reads [[commit]](https://github.com/OffchainLabs/nitro/commit/8512b8cbf477b35e9717f80fb4b32ebe6ea30167)

```

PROTOCOL_UPGRADE: FALSE
DATABASE_UPGRADE: FALSE

```

This release includes #15969 and #15975 on top of 2.13.0-rc.1.

This a stable release suitable for Testnet, Devnet and Mainnet Beta.

https://github.com/anza-xyz/agave/blob/v4.1/CHANGELOG.md

## What's Changed
* v4.1: Create a set of slots with zero lamport accounts to shrink after full snapshot (backport of #12665) by @mergify[bot] in https://github.com/anza-xyz/agave/pull/13097
* v4.1: ci: use github app token in version bump (backport of #13136) by @mergify[bot] in https://github.com/anza-xyz/agave/pull/13260

## Who should upgrade

Fullnodes: REQUIRED

* [trading-native][types] Add native-position StateKey variant + per-block NativeTransactionWrites by @grao1991 in https://github.com/aptos-labs/aptos-core/pull/19737
* [mempool] add pull_count label and prev-block latency metric by @zekun000 in https://github.com/aptos-labs/aptos-core/pull/19771
* [backpressure] tuning per block gas limit by @zekun000 in https://github.com/aptos-labs/aptos-core/pull/19770
* [cherry-pick][vm] Prevent Display recursion stack overflow on deep Values (main) by @vgao1996 in https://github.com/aptos-labs/aptos-core/pull/19774
* [forge] bump deployer version by @aptos-bot in https://github.com/aptos-labs/aptos-core/pull/19579
* [metrics] Add block-time and txn-cut metrics by @danielxiangzl in https://github.com/aptos-labs/aptos-core/pull/19741
* Sync Terraform & Helm by @aptos-bot in https://github.com/aptos-labs/aptos-core/pull/19215
* [forge] bump deployer version by @aptos-bot in https://github.com/aptos-labs/aptos-core/pull/19783
* Add readable events and changes output to move simulation by @WGB5445 in https://github.com/aptos-labs/aptos-core/pull/19273
* [docker] document trusted setup files in validator compose by @ibalajiarun in https://github.com/aptos-labs/aptos-core/pull/19768
* [move] …[truncated]

![image](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/berachain/beacon-kit/refs/heads/main/.github/assets/banner.png)

## Summary

This is the **mainnet-ready Fusaka (Fulu) release** for BeaconKit. It is the first stable cut of the `v1.4.x` line and bundles the full Fulu fork along with a round of CometBFT security fixes and node API hardening.

- **Mainnet Fusaka (Fulu) hard fork.** Sets the mainnet Fulu fork time and ships everything the fork requires: the [BRIP-0008](https://github.com/berachain/BRIPs) effective-balance hysteresis update, EIP-6110 style in-protocol deposit processing, the new Fulu EVM inflation values, and corrected EL genesis configs. **All mainnet operators must upgrade to this version before the fork activates.**
- **CometBFT security fixes.** Bumps CometBFT to a version that includes additional consensus-layer fixes, on top of the earlier `maxPeerHeight` poisoning fix.
- **Node API hardening.** Bounds previously unbounded user input on the filter and proof APIs (validator index / filter inputs), closing a resource-exhaustion vector.
- **Engine & resilience fixes.** The engine client now classifies EL HTTP 4xx responses as fatal via a typed `HTTPStatusError`, and retries on EL connection errors to survive execution-client restarts.
- **Performance.** Adds a validator set cache to avoid a full state scan in `GetValidators`.

This version is **required for the mainnet Fusaka hard fork** occurring at **July 8th 16:00:00 UTC** (unix `1783526400`). The …[truncated]

## What Changed

> [!IMPORTANT]
> **Base Mainnet**: Operators must upgrade to this release by June 25th at 18:00:00 UTC for the Beryl hardfork. See [here](https://docs.base.org/base-chain/specs/upgrades/beryl/overview) for more information.
> **Base Sepolia**: Recommended upgrade to improve Flashblocks stability

### base-reth-node
- Beryl Support for Mainnet
- Fix Flashblock processing where transactions relying on the BLOCKHASH could have different execution outcomes during Flashblocks and canonical processing.

### base-consensus
- Beryl Support for Mainnet

> [!Warning]
> **This is a mandatory release for all node operators.** It includes the [Mainnet](https://github.com/ethereum-optimism/superchain-registry/blob/c90d86d2ed13541f16daf5ce8707c688723ca039/superchain/configs/mainnet/op.toml#L24) timestamps for the Karst hardfork.
>
> The following Mainnet chains will activate on **1783526401 (Wed Jul 8 2026 16:00:01 UTC)**, pending governance approval: op, ink, zora, mode, lisk, metal, unichain, soneium, ethernity, tbn
>
> **Reminder:** op-geth has reached [end of support](https://docs.optimism.io/notices/op-geth-deprecation), so you must be running op-reth to support the karst activation. For more details on this network upgrade see our [notice page](https://docs.optimism.io/notices/upgrade-19).

> [!IMPORTANT]
> **Operators must use op-node/v1.19.1 with this release.** Prior versions of op-node will not work with this version of op-reth after Karst due to a new engine API method (`getPayloadV5`) used after Karst. See the [notice page](https://docs.optimism.io/notices/upgrade-19) for more details about recommended versions (including flashblocks components).

op-reth can now load OP Sepolia and OP Mainnet timestamps from the superchain registry. If you are a node operator for either of those chains, you do not need to specify a custom genesis file with the `--chain` flag.

> [!CAUTION]
> **Mandatory upgrade.** Fixes eth_estimateGas after the Karst hardfork.

## What's Changed in op-reth/v2.3.3
* fix(op-reth): set …[truncated]

Bor v2.8.3 is a maintenance release containing bug fixes across the miner and consensus engine, a full gRPC server implementation (disabled by default), a builder-phase streaming prefetcher, and observability, configuration, and build improvements.

## What's Changed

### Features

* (feat): disable pending block creation loop via flag by @manav2401 in https://github.com/0xPolygon/bor/pull/2175
* consensus/bor, internal/cli: full grpc implementation by @marcello33 in https://github.com/0xPolygon/bor/pull/2194
* prefetcher: builder-phase prefetch + streaming worker pool by @lucca30 in https://github.com/0xPolygon/bor/pull/2192
* (feat): tracing support for state-sync transactions by @manav2401 in https://github.com/0xPolygon/bor/pull/2236
* security: harden gRPC server — disable by default, bind to localhost by @harshinsecurity in https://github.com/0xPolygon/bor/pull/2078

### Bug Fixes

* consensus: fix milestone-mismatch rewind deadlock by @kamuikatsurgi in https://github.com/0xPolygon/bor/pull/2246
* consensus/bor, miner: fix stale pending state for non-validator nodes by @cffls in https://github.com/0xPolygon/bor/pull/2183
* miner, consensus/bor: fix leaked-wedge family in worker state machine by @cffls in https://github.com/0xPolygon/bor/pull/2220
* miner: drop PeerCount==0 gate in mainLoop by @cffls in https://github.com/0xPolygon/bor/pull/2238
* consensus/bor: fix race in SpanStore.PurgeCache by @lucca30 in https://github.com/0xPolygon/bor/pull/2235
* miner: disable …[truncated]

This is a **non-mandatory** upgrade for mainnet, but **highly recommended** for all validators. It is **required for validators running the giga executor**.

No operator action is required beyond upgrading the binary.

## Highlights

**Giga executor: robust fallback for unsupported store iteration** ([#3563](https://github.com/sei-protocol/sei-chain/pull/3563))

Fixes a case where giga nodes could diverge from v2 nodes and halt. Certain transactions (such as `SELFDESTRUCT` and contract creation over an existing account) trigger store iteration that the giga executor does not support. These transactions now fall back cleanly to the v2 executor, keeping giga-enabled validators byte-for-byte consistent with v2 nodes.

## What's Changed
* fix(giga): robust handling of unsupported iterators (#3560) by @arajasek in https://github.com/sei-protocol/sei-chain/pull/3563
* Update changelog in prep to release v6.5.2 by @masih in https://github.com/sei-protocol/sei-chain/pull/3569


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/sei-protocol/sei-chain/compare/v6.5.1...v6.5.2

governance.title: [Frax] [FIP-449] EchoMarket Creator & Distribution Program for Frax Finance
governance.body:
Quorum: 6.63m CVX 



**Authors:**
EchoMarket Team

## **Summary**

This proposal requests **42,000 wFRAX** over a 6-month period to expand Frax Finance's creator and distribution network through EchoMarket.

Budget allocation:

* **30,000 wFRAX** for Frax Force (**5,000 wFRAX/month**)

* **12,000 wFRAX** for the Frax Creator & Distributor Program (**2,000 wFRAX/month**)

The goal is to increase awareness of Frax products, strengthen ecosystem mindshare, and build a sustainable network of creators producing educational and community-focused content around Frax.

---

## **Background**

EchoMarket started in September 2024 and launched publicly on Fraxtal in June 2025.

Since launch, EchoMarket has achieved:

* **2.5M+ validated impressions**

* **250+ active creators**

* Recurring campaigns for Frax Finance and ecosystem partners

* AI-powered content verification and scoring

The original EchoMarket grant helped establish Frax Force and onboard a growing community of creators focused on Frax content and ecosystem awareness.

---

## **Why This Matters**

Frax has built one of the strongest product suites in DeFi, but continued growth requires distribution.

Creators, educators, …[truncated]

---

## **Background**

EchoMarket started in September 2024 and launched publicly on Fraxtal in June 2025.

Since launch, EchoMarket has achieved:

* **2.5M+ validated impressions**

* **250+ active creators**

* Recurring campaigns for Frax Finance and ecosystem partners

* AI-powered content verification and scoring

The original EchoMarket grant helped establish Frax Force and onboard a growing community of creators focused on Frax content and ecosystem awareness.

---

## **Why This Matters**

Frax has built one of the strongest product suites in DeFi, but continued growth requires distribution.

Creators, educators, researchers, and …[truncated]

governance.title: [Temp Check] - Update Crosschain Governance Parameters for Avalanche, MegaETH, Soneium, and X Layer
governance.body:
## Summary

Secure crosschain messaging is an integral part of Uniswap's governance model. Governance votes like protocol fee adjustments are executed by UNI holders on Ethereum Mainnet and must subsequently be relayed to destination chains.

This proposal updates Uniswap's crosschain governance system to accommodate the latest best practices. Specifically, we propose to:

-   Transition ownership of the Uniswap v2 and v4 contracts on Soneium and X Layer to CrossChainAccount contracts, which we consider to be the current best practice for executing messages from Ethereum Mainnet on the OP Stack
    
-   Migrate the entire messaging system for the Avalanche and MegaETH deployments from LayerZero v1 (part of which is [being deprecated](https://layerzero.network/blog/ongoing-security-updates)) to Wormhole
    

Note that because Uniswap v3 on both Soneium and X Layer is owned by the v3OpenFeeAdapter, which is owned by the CrossChainAccount, we do not need to change the parameter on v3 on these chains.

---

## Specification

### Current Configuration - Avalanche and MegaETH

Both chains currently use LayerZero v1 for governance messaging. The OmnichainProposalSender contract exists …[truncated]

governance.title: Term 7 Meta-Governance Working Group Election
governance.body:
This is a vote to elect three stewards for the Meta-Governance Working Group for the Term 7 (July 1, 2026 - June 30, 2027).

This election will be using ranked-choice voting, with Copeland (a very precise algorithm for computing collective preferences) and Shielded Voting (your vote will be private during the voting window and revealed afterward).

To vote, rank each candidate in your order of preference.

At the conclusion of voting, the three highest-ranked candidates will be elected as stewards of the Meta-Governance Working Group for Term 7.

A list of candidates, with links to their nomination statement and Twitter profile, is included below. The list is presented in the order in which candidates submitted their nominations in the ENS DAO governance forum.

## Candidates

**James / jkm.eth**
[Nomination Statement](https://discuss.ens.domains/t/meta-governance-working-group-steward-nominations-term-7-2026/22157/3) / [X Profile](https://x.com/jkm__eth)

[Nomination Statement](https://discuss.ens.domains/t/meta-governance-working-group-steward-nominations-term-7-2026/22157/4) / [X Profile](https://x.com/sovereignsignal)

**vegayp.eth**
[Nomination …[truncated]

governance.title: AGV Wind-Down: Structured Transition & Return of Capital to the DAO Treasury
governance.body:
## Abstract
*Co-authors: AGV Team, OpCo Team, Arbitrum Foundation & OAT*

This proposal seeks DAO approval for AGV to cease forward investment activity and return surplus capital to the DAO treasury for redeployment. Following a broader review of the Arbitrum ecosystem, the OAT, AGV, and other relevant stakeholders have determined that continuing to operate AGV as a consumer- and gaming-focused venture platform is no longer aligned with the DAO's current priorities. Accordingly, AGV's scope will be narrowed to focus exclusively on managing and maximising the value of the existing portfolio, including selective follow-on investments. Its grant program and any new capital investments outside the portfolio will be sunset.

Under this proposal, ~143.7 million ARB currently held for the AGV program out of the 225.0 million originally allocated will be returned to the DAO treasury. Remaining assets will be reviewed to support the existing portfolio, maintain follow-on reserves, satisfy outstanding obligations, and fund ongoing portfolio management through the end of 2026. Any surplus capital will also be returned to the DAO treasury.

To execute this, the proposal tasks the Arbitrum …[truncated]

governance.title: [Constitutional] AIP: Automate Timeboost Proceeds Split
governance.body:
## Abstract

This proposal seeks approval to automate the Timeboost proceeds split on Arbitrum One and Arbitrum Nova, and distribute accrued rewards to the Arbitrum Developer Guild (ADG) via a constitutional vote, as per the already-passed and executed [proposal to adopt Timeboost](https://forum.arbitrum.foundation/t/constitutional-aip-proposal-to-adopt-timeboost-a-new-transaction-ordering-policy/25167). Currently, all Timeboost bid proceeds are sent to the [Arbitrum DAO treasury](https://arbiscan.io/address/0xbFc1FECa8B09A5c5D3EFfE7429eBE24b9c09EF58); the portion of Timeboost proceeds allocated to the ADG (3%) must also be sent via an onchain action. This process can be automated by introducing a fee splitter that forwards funds proportionally to the DAO Treasury and to the ADG address that is controlled by the Arbitrum Foundation (AF).

The ADG was established to recognize and support long-term contributors whose work would strengthen the Arbitrum technology, tooling, and infrastructure. For a more detailed update on the ADG, see page 27 in the [AF’s 2025 Transparency …[truncated]
